tables.name: ActionAuditLog
tables.description: Centralized audit trail for all user-initiated actions in the Control Center. Captures configuration changes, schedule edits, job triggers, BDL imports, access grants, alert resends, and any other operational action performed through the UI.
tables.dataFlow: Populated by Control Center API route handlers whenever a user performs an action through any CC page. Each discrete action generates one row with a human-readable summary. Rows are append-only and never updated or deleted.

columns.name: audit_id
columns.description: Primary key identity.

columns.name: page_route
columns.description: Control Center page route where the action originated (e.g., /admin, /bdl-import, /apps-int).

columns.name: action_type
columns.description: Action category: CONFIG_CHANGE, SCHEDULE_CHANGE, JOB_TRIGGER, BDL_IMPORT, ACCESS_CHANGE, ALERT_RESEND.

columns.name: action_summary
columns.description: Human-readable description of what happened, built by the calling code to capture essential context in a single string.

columns.name: environment
columns.description: Target DM environment for environment-scoped actions (TEST, STAGE, PROD). NULL for actions that are not environment-specific.

columns.name: result
columns.description: Outcome for operational actions: SUCCESS or FAILED. NULL for instant actions like config changes.

columns.name: error_detail
columns.description: Error message captured on failure. NULL on success or for action types where result is not tracked.

columns.name: executed_by
columns.description: AD username of the user who performed the action (FAC\ domain prefix).

columns.name: executed_dttm
columns.description: Timestamp when the action was performed.

designNotes.note: Each row captures a single user action with a human-readable action_summary string built by the calling code. The summary includes all relevant context (e.g., "Changed orchestrator_drain_mode from 0 to 1" or "Triggered Refresh Drools (3 servers)"). This avoids structured old/new value columns in favor of flexibility across diverse action types.

Operational actions with cooldown periods (e.g., Balance Sync at 60 minutes) query the most recent successful execution by action_type, action_summary pattern, and environment to determine eligibility. The table serves as both audit trail and throttle source.

statusValues.meaning: A configuration setting was modified through a Control Center administrative interface.
A DM scheduled job or process was manually triggered through the Control Center.

queries.name: Recent actions across all pages
queries.description: Shows the most recent user actions with full context.
queries.sql:
SELECT TOP 50 page_route, action_type, action_summary,
       environment, result, error_detail,
       executed_by, executed_dttm
FROM dbo.ActionAuditLog
ORDER BY executed_dttm DESC;

queries.name: Actions by type
queries.description: Filtered audit history for a specific action category.
queries.sql:
SELECT action_summary, environment, result, error_detail,
       executed_by, executed_dttm
FROM dbo.ActionAuditLog
WHERE action_type = 'JOB_TRIGGER'
ORDER BY executed_dttm DESC;

queries.name: Cooldown check for job triggers
queries.description: Finds the most recent successful execution of a specific trigger in an environment.
queries.sql:
SELECT TOP 1 executed_dttm
FROM dbo.ActionAuditLog
WHERE action_type = 'JOB_TRIGGER'
  AND action_summary LIKE '%Balance Sync%'
  AND environment = 'PROD'
  AND result = 'SUCCESS'
ORDER BY executed_dttm DESC;

relationshipNotes.note: CONFIG_CHANGE entries originating from GlobalConfig edits on the Admin page. Action summary includes the setting name and old/new values.

tables.name: API_RequestLog
tables.description: Tracks API request metrics for volume and performance analysis. Initially captures Control Center API traffic with extensibility for future API sources.
tables.dataFlow: Populated automatically by Pode middleware in the Control Center on every API request completion. Each row captures the endpoint, HTTP method, caller identity, response timing, and status code. The logging middleware is designed to fail silently rather than impact actual API requests. Request and response bodies are intentionally excluded to avoid storage bloat and sensitivity concerns.

columns.name: request_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the log entry

columns.name: endpoint
columns.description: The API path called (e.g., /api/backup/pipeline-status)

columns.name: http_method
columns.description: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)

columns.name: user_name
columns.description: Authenticated user name from AD

columns.name: client_ip
columns.description: Client IP address (supports IPv6)

columns.name: user_agent
columns.description: User-Agent header - identifies browser, script, or application

columns.name: request_dttm
columns.description: When the request was processed

columns.name: duration_ms
columns.description: Total processing time in milliseconds

columns.name: status_code
columns.description: HTTP response status code (200, 404, 500, etc.)

columns.name: response_bytes
columns.description: Size of response body in bytes

columns.name: source_application
columns.description: Application that logged the request (e.g., ControlCenter)

designNotes.note: The source_application column (currently 'ControlCenter' for all rows) allows future expansion to log API traffic from multiple applications in a single table for unified analysis.

The logging middleware catches all exceptions internally and never throws. Logging failures must not impact the actual API request being processed. Observability should never cause the problem it's trying to detect.

queries.name: Request volume by endpoint (last 24 hours)
queries.description: Shows traffic patterns and performance per endpoint.
queries.sql:
SELECT endpoint, COUNT(*) AS request_count,
       AVG(duration_ms) AS avg_duration_ms,
       MAX(duration_ms) AS max_duration_ms
FROM dbo.API_RequestLog
WHERE request_dttm >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, GETDATE())
GROUP BY endpoint
ORDER BY request_count DESC;

queries.name: Slowest endpoints (last 24 hours)
queries.description: Identifies performance bottlenecks by average response time.
queries.sql:
SELECT endpoint, COUNT(*) AS request_count,
       AVG(duration_ms) AS avg_duration_ms,
       MAX(duration_ms) AS max_duration_ms
FROM dbo.API_RequestLog
WHERE request_dttm >= DATEADD(DAY, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY endpoint
ORDER BY avg_duration_ms DESC;

tables.name: Asset_Registry
tables.description: Catalog of every component (CSS class, JS function, HTML ID, API route, etc.) extracted from Control Center source files. One row per definition or usage instance. Distinguishes local from shared scope and maps consumption to definition. Serves as both descriptive catalog (what exists, where) and prescriptive reference (naming conventions, established patterns). Populated by parser scripts that walk all CC source files, parse them via language-appropriate AST tools (PostCSS for CSS, Acorn for JS, built-in PowerShell parser for .ps1/.psm1), and produce one row per extracted component instance. Refresh strategy is truncate-and-reload per file_type, reflecting current state only with no historical retention.

columns.name: asset_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key. IDENTITY column.

columns.name: file_name
columns.description: Name of the source file the row was extracted from. For HTML extraction this is the .ps1 or .psm1 file containing the markup; the file_type column distinguishes HTML extraction from PS function extraction within the same physical file.

columns.name: object_registry_id
columns.description: Foreign key reference to dbo.Object_Registry for the file this row was extracted from. Allows joining to the registered CC file for component and module context.

columns.name: file_type
columns.description: Source-language classification of the row. See status_value entries for valid values.

columns.name: line_start
columns.description: Starting line number in the source file where this component instance begins. 1-based.

columns.name: line_end
columns.description: Ending line number in the source file. Same as line_start for single-line items.

columns.name: column_start
columns.description: Starting column number on line_start where this component instance begins. 1-based. NULL when column information is not available from the parser.

columns.name: component_type
columns.description: Type of component this row represents. See status_value entries for valid values.

columns.name: component_subtype
columns.description: Optional finer-grained typing for component_type values that benefit from sub-classification.

columns.name: component_name
columns.description: Name of the component (e.g., xf-modal for a CSS class, escapeHtml for a JS function, /api/bidata/todays-build for an API route). For COMMENT_BANNER rows, contains the banner title.

columns.name: state_modifier
columns.description: For CSS_CLASS rows: comma-separated list of compound state modifier classes attached to the primary class. Example: a CSS rule .slide-panel.wide.open produces one DEFINITION row with component_name=slide-panel and state_modifier=wide, open.

columns.name: reference_type
columns.description: Whether this row represents a definition or a usage. See status_value entries for valid values.

columns.name: scope
columns.description: Whether this component lives in shared infrastructure or is page-local. See status_value entries for valid values.

columns.name: source_file
columns.description: The file where this component is defined. Same as file_name for DEFINITION rows. For USAGE rows: points at the file where the component being used is defined. See status_value entries for special values.

columns.name: source_section
columns.description: Title of the COMMENT_BANNER section that contains this component, if any. Populated by the parser based on the most recent banner seen above the component during extraction.

columns.name: signature
columns.description: Full source signature of the component. For CSS rows: the complete selector text including all selectors in a multi-selector list. For JS function rows: the function declaration including parameters. For HTML rows: the full attribute text. VARCHAR(MAX).

columns.name: parent_function
columns.description: Parent context for nested components. For CSS rules nested inside @media or @supports: the at-rule label. For JS functions nested inside other functions: the parent function name. NULL when the component is at top level.

columns.name: parent_object
columns.description: Optional manual cross-reference to another related object.

columns.name: raw_text
columns.description: Raw source snippet for the component. VARCHAR(MAX).

columns.name: purpose_description
columns.description: Human-readable description of the component purpose. Captures intent context that source code alone cannot convey.

columns.name: design_notes
columns.description: Free-form design notes about the component. Captures non-obvious decisions, gotchas, naming-pattern guidance, cross-references to related components.

columns.name: related_asset_id
columns.description: Self-referencing pointer to another Asset_Registry row.

columns.name: occurrence_index
columns.description: Per-file ordinal counter that disambiguates multiple instances of the same (file_name, component_type, component_name, reference_type) tuple. The Nth occurrence in source order gets occurrence_index N. Forms part of the natural key for cross-references.

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Active flag. Default 1. Used to manually exclude rows from reports without deleting them.

columns.name: first_parsed_dttm
columns.description: Timestamp of when this row was first inserted into the table. Default SYSDATETIME() at insert.

columns.name: last_parsed_dttm
columns.description: Timestamp of when this row was last confirmed by the parser. Default SYSDATETIME() at insert.

checkConstraints.name: CK_Asset_Registry_component_type
checkConstraints.definition: ([component_type]='COMMENT_BANNER' OR [component_type]='HTML_ID' OR [component_type]='API_ROUTE' OR [component_type]='PS_ASSIGNMENT' OR [component_type]='PS_COMMAND' OR [component_type]='PS_PARAM' OR [component_type]='PS_FUNCTION' OR [component_type]='JS_HOOK' OR [component_type]='JS_CONSTANT' OR [component_type]='JS_FUNCTION' OR [component_type]='CSS_KEYFRAME' OR [component_type]='CSS_VARIABLE' OR [component_type]='CSS_CLASS' OR [component_type]='CSS_RULE')

designNotes.note: The table is fully truncated and reloaded by the parser pipeline on each refresh cycle, rather than maintained via incremental MERGE upsert. The catalog represents current state only — there is no historical retention of removed components. This decision favors operational simplicity and query clarity (every row is, by definition, currently present in the source) over the complexity of incremental update logic. Manual annotations (purpose_description, design_notes, related_asset_id) are not preserved across truncate cycles via the asset_id; instead, manual annotations are layered on via a separate annotations mechanism keyed on the natural key.

The natural key is (file_name, component_type, component_name, reference_type, occurrence_index). Surrogate asset_id values are not stable across parser runs because of the truncate-and-reload model; cross-references from other tables or annotation systems must use the natural key. The occurrence_index column exists specifically to disambiguate multiple instances of the same component within a single file (e.g., the 1st, 2nd, 3rd USAGE of xf-modal in BIDATAMonitoring.ps1).

A shared component used on multiple pages produces multiple USAGE rows in this table — one per consumer location — rather than being normalized into a definition table plus a separate dependency table. This deliberate choice allows a single query to return the complete picture for any page or any component without joins. A definition is queryable via reference_type=DEFINITION; consumption is queryable via reference_type=USAGE; promotion of a local utility to shared is a clean batch UPDATE on scope and source_file rather than an insert/delete dance.

CSS DEFINITION rows have scope determined by which file declares them: SHARED if the file is in a curated shared-files list (currently engine-events.css), LOCAL otherwise. HTML USAGE rows have scope determined by cross-reference: SHARED if the class has any SHARED CSS DEFINITION row in the catalog, LOCAL if only LOCAL DEFINITION rows exist, LOCAL with source_file=<undefined> if no DEFINITION exists in any cataloged file. This means HTML extraction depends on CSS extraction having already run; the parser pipeline orchestrator enforces this ordering.

statusValues.meaning: A CSS class. DEFINITION rows from CSS files (a CSS rule defines the class), USAGE rows from CSS files (a CSS rule consumes the class via descendant selector) or HTML files (an HTML class= attribute applies the class).
Row was extracted from a .css file. component_name typically refers to a CSS class, variable, keyframe, or rule.

The row represents the place where the component is defined: the CSS rule that creates a class, the JS function declaration, the @keyframes rule, the Add-PodeRoute call, the id="..." attribute. The source_file column points at the file containing the definition.

The component lives in shared platform infrastructure. For DEFINITION rows: the component is defined in a curated shared file (currently engine-events.css, engine-events.js, engine-events-API.ps1, xFACts-Helpers.psm1). For USAGE rows: the component being consumed has a SHARED DEFINITION somewhere in the catalog.

Special source_file value indicating the component is referenced in HTML markup but has no DEFINITION in any cataloged source file. Most commonly applies to state modifier classes applied as space-separated HTML classes (active, disabled, hidden), classes used only via JS class manipulation, or dead references from prior code that has not been cleaned up.

The component is page-specific. For DEFINITION rows: the component is defined in a page-specific file. For USAGE rows: the component being consumed is only defined locally, or has no DEFINITION at all in the catalog (in which case source_file is <undefined>).

The row represents a place where the component is used: a CSS rule referencing a class via descendant selector, an HTML class= attribute consuming a class, a JS animation property referencing a keyframe, a fetch() call hitting an API route. The source_file column points at the file where the consumed component is defined (which may be a different file from file_name).
Row was extracted from a .js file by parsing the JavaScript AST. component_name typically refers to a JS function, constant, or hook.
A CSS custom property (e.g., --portal-bg). DEFINITION rows for the property declaration, USAGE rows for var() references.
A @keyframes rule (e.g., pulse, spin). DEFINITION rows for the rule declaration, USAGE rows for animation property references.
Row was extracted from PowerShell function or route definitions in a .ps1 or .psm1 file. component_name typically refers to a PS function, parameter, command, assignment, or API route.

Row was extracted from HTML markup embedded in a .ps1 or .psm1 file (in here-strings or string tokens). component_name typically refers to a CSS class consumed by the markup or an HTML id attribute. file_name points at the .ps1/.psm1 file containing the markup.
A non-class CSS rule (e.g., body, *, ::-webkit-scrollbar). DEFINITION only.

A section header comment in a source file (a block comment containing 5 or more = characters as a separator rule). DEFINITION only. The banner title becomes the component_name and serves as source_section context for subsequent rows in the same file.
An id attribute occurrence in HTML markup. DEFINITION only (an id="..." attribute defines an ID for the page). HTML_IDs are inherently page-local.
A JavaScript function declaration or expression assigned to a name. DEFINITION rows for the declaration, USAGE rows for call sites.
A JavaScript top-level const, let, or var declaration. DEFINITION rows for the declaration, USAGE rows for references.

A JavaScript function the page defines for the shared module to invoke (e.g., onPageRefresh, onSessionExpired, onEngineProcessCompleted). DEFINITION rows for the page-side declaration, USAGE rows for the shared-module-side invocation.
A PowerShell function definition. DEFINITION rows for the function declaration, USAGE rows for call sites.
A PowerShell parameter declaration on a function or script-level param block.
A PowerShell command invocation worth cataloging (e.g., calls to platform helpers like Invoke-XFActsQuery, Send-TeamsAlert).
A PowerShell module-scope or script-scope variable assignment.
A Pode route definition (an Add-PodeRoute call in a CC API .ps1 file). DEFINITION rows for the route handler, USAGE rows for client-side fetch() calls or ajax requests targeting the route.

tables.name: ClientHierarchy
tables.description: Complete flattened DM creditor hierarchy providing single-lookup resolution from any creditor to its direct parent group and ultimate top-level parent. Rebuilt daily by Sync-ClientHierarchy.ps1 using a recursive CTE against crs5_oltp creditor and creditor group tables. Standalone creditors (crdtr_grp_id = 1) self-reference — their parent and top parent fields point to themselves. Includes all creditors regardless of transaction history or active status.
tables.dataFlow: Sync-ClientHierarchy.ps1 rebuilds the entire table daily via MERGE, reading from crs5_oltp.dbo.crdtr and crs5_oltp.dbo.crdtr_grp on the AG. The recursive CTE resolves the full creditor group hierarchy in a single pass. The B2B module uses this table to resolve Integration CREDITOR_NAME values (CE/CB codes) to the DM client hierarchy for crosswalk and grouping operations.

columns.name: creditor_id
columns.description: DM creditor identifier (crdtr_id from crs5_oltp.dbo.crdtr). Primary key.

columns.name: creditor_key
columns.description: DM creditor short name (crdtr_shrt_nm) — the CE/CB code used as the crosswalk key to B2B CREDITOR_NAME.

columns.name: creditor_name
columns.description: DM creditor display name (crdtr_nm).

columns.name: parent_group_id
columns.description: Direct parent creditor group identifier. Self-references creditor_id for standalone creditors (crdtr_grp_id = 1).

columns.name: parent_group_key
columns.description: Direct parent group short name. Self-references creditor_key for standalone creditors.

columns.name: parent_group_name
columns.description: Direct parent group display name. Self-references creditor_name for standalone creditors.

columns.name: top_parent_id
columns.description: Highest ancestor creditor group identifier resolved via recursive CTE. Self-references creditor_id for standalone creditors.

columns.name: top_parent_key
columns.description: Highest ancestor group short name. Self-references creditor_key for standalone creditors.

columns.name: top_parent_name
columns.description: Highest ancestor group display name. Self-references creditor_name for standalone creditors.

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Creditor active status derived from crdtr_stts_cd = 1 in crs5_oltp.

columns.name: parent_group_is_active
columns.description: Direct parent group active status derived from crdtr_grp_sft_dlt_flg in crs5_oltp (N = active, Y = soft-deleted). For standalone creditors (self-referencing), mirrors the creditor is_active flag. Enables detection of active creditors assigned to inactive groups.

columns.name: top_parent_is_active
columns.description: Top-level parent group active status derived from crdtr_grp_sft_dlt_flg at the highest ancestor level. For standalone creditors (self-referencing), mirrors the creditor is_active flag. Combined with parent_group_is_active and is_active, enables full hierarchy health assessment.

columns.name: last_refreshed_dttm
columns.description: Timestamp of the most recent sync cycle that wrote or confirmed this row.

designNotes.note: Creditors assigned to crdtr_grp_id = 1 (the internal default group) are standalone — they have no meaningful group membership. Rather than NULLing the parent and top parent columns, these creditors self-reference: their parent_group and top_parent fields point back to their own creditor_id, creditor_key, and creditor_name. This avoids NULL handling in every consumer query and allows consistent GROUP BY top_parent_name behavior.

The CTE anchor excludes crdtr_grp_id = 1 (DefGrp / Internal Creditor Group) to prevent duplicate path resolution. Group 1 is a system default, not a real parent. Including it would create false hierarchy paths for creditors that happen to be in the default group.

This table includes ALL creditors regardless of transaction history or recency. This differs from the legacy Jira_ClientTblRanked table which filters to 13 months of activity. Full population ensures the crosswalk works for any creditor the B2B system references, even inactive or dormant ones.

relationshipNotes.note: Rebuild script that performs the full MERGE. Reads crs5_oltp.dbo.crdtr and crs5_oltp.dbo.crdtr_grp via the AG listener. Registered in ProcessRegistry for daily execution.
The B2B module will use ClientHierarchy to resolve Integration CREDITOR_NAME (CE/CB codes) from CLIENTS_ACCTS to the full DM hierarchy for client grouping and display.

tables.name: Component_Registry
tables.description: Catalog of logical components in the xFACts platform. Each component groups related database objects, scripts, and Control Center files into a single versioned unit. Component_Registry defines what groupings exist; Object_Registry holds the individual object membership; System_Metadata tracks version history against these components.
tables.dataFlow: Rows are inserted when new components are defined during platform development. Referenced by Object_Registry (FK on component_name) and System_Metadata (FK on component_name). The Admin page System Metadata modal reads Component_Registry to display the component tree and allow version bumps. New components can be added via the Admin UI. The doc_* columns drive the documentation pipeline — a JSON export (doc-registry.json) is generated from rows where doc_page_id is populated and consumed by the site navigation, Hub card grid, and documentation publisher.

columns.name: component_id
columns.description: Auto-incrementing primary key.

columns.name: module_name
columns.description: Functional module this component belongs to: dbo, ServerOps, JobFlow, BatchOps, BIDATA, FileOps, Teams, Jira, Orchestrator, ControlCenter, DeptOps.

columns.name: component_name
columns.description: Unique component identifier using dot notation for scoped components (e.g., ServerOps.Backup, ControlCenter.Admin) or plain name for module-level components (e.g., JobFlow, Teams).

columns.name: description
columns.description: Brief description of what this component encompasses — the functional scope and purpose.

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Soft delete flag. 1 = active component, 0 = retired/decommissioned.

columns.name: doc_page_id
columns.description: Unique page identifier used by the documentation pipeline to derive filenames, build navigation, and link consumers to pages. NULL for components without documentation pages. Multiple components can share the same doc_page_id when they contribute sections to the same page.

columns.name: doc_title
columns.description: Display title for the page — used in site navigation, Hub card grid, and published documentation. Only populated on the primary row for each page (the row with doc_sort_order set). NULL for secondary rows on multi-component pages and for components without documentation pages.

columns.name: doc_json_schema
columns.description: Schema name(s) for the JSON DDL reference data consumed by the reference page. Comma-separated when multiple schemas contribute. Maps to JSON filenames in the documentation data directory. NULL when the component does not contribute objects to a reference page.

columns.name: doc_json_categories
columns.description: Category filter(s) applied when the component uses only a subset of a schema's objects on the reference page. Comma-separated. Filters the JSON data by the category field in Object_Metadata. NULL when no filtering is needed or no reference page contribution exists.

columns.name: doc_cc_slug
columns.description: Named CC guide page slug. When populated, this component has a dedicated CC guide page at {pageId}-cc-{slug}.html in the cc/ subfolder. The doc_title on this same row provides the nav label. When NULL, the standard single-file convention {pageId}-cc.html applies. Only activates when at least one section row for the pageId has a slug — backward compatible with all existing single-CC-guide pages.

columns.name: doc_sort_order
columns.description: Display order for page position in navigation and the index card grid. Lower values appear first. Uses increments of 10 for easy insertion of new pages. A value of 0 identifies the index page (site root). Only populated on the primary row for each page. NULL for secondary rows and components without documentation pages.

columns.name: doc_section_order
columns.description: Display order for this component's section within a multi-component reference page. Controls the sequence of schema sections in the reference page navigation. For single-component pages, this is 1. NULL for the Hub and components without documentation pages.

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When this component was registered. Auto-populated via default.

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who registered this component. Auto-populated via SUSER_SNAME() default.

designNotes.note: Components use dot notation when a module has multiple components (ServerOps.Backup, ServerOps.Index, ControlCenter.Admin). Module-level components where the module has only one component use the plain module name (JobFlow, Teams, BatchOps). This provides natural grouping in sorted displays while keeping names concise.

designNotes.summary: How Component_Registry, Object_Registry, and System_Metadata work together.
designNotes.note: Component_Registry defines the logical groupings. Object_Registry catalogs every individual object and links it to its parent component. System_Metadata tracks version history per component. The component_name column is the natural join key across all three tables. A component exists because it has a row here; its contents are in Object_Registry; its version history is in System_Metadata.

The doc_* columns consolidate three previously independent page registries into a single database-driven source. A JSON export (doc-registry.json) is generated by the documentation pipeline and consumed by the site navigation, the Hub card grid, and the documentation publisher. Adding a new documentation page requires only populating the doc_* columns on the component row and re-running the pipeline — no code changes needed.

Filenames are derived from doc_page_id by convention with standard suffixes. Child page existence is determined by filesystem check — if the file exists in the expected directory, the nav renders the link. New page types can be added by establishing a suffix convention without any schema changes.

Multiple components can share the same doc_page_id when they contribute sections to the same page. One component is the primary row (has doc_sort_order and doc_title populated). Additional components are secondary rows with only doc_page_id, doc_json_schema, doc_json_categories, and doc_section_order populated. The reference page groups and orders sections by doc_section_order.

designNotes.summary: Sort order convention replaces dedicated flag column.
designNotes.note: The documentation site index page is identified by doc_sort_order = 0. All module pages use increments of 10 starting at 10. This convention eliminates the need for a separate boolean column and is enforced by the documentation pipeline consumers (nav.js, publisher, JSON export). Only one row should have sort order 0.

The standard documentation convention is one CC guide page per pageId ({pageId}-cc.html). When a pageId needs multiple CC guide pages — because its Control Center presence spans multiple distinct pages with different functionality — the doc_cc_slug column enables named pages ({pageId}-cc-{slug}.html). The presence of any non-NULL slug for a pageId suppresses the standard single-file check in nav.js, preventing a confusing mix of generic and named links. Slug rows must also have doc_title populated for nav labels, and doc_page_id set to the parent pageId.

statusValues.meaning: Component is active and in use. Default value on INSERT.
Component has been retired or decommissioned. Objects may still exist in Object_Registry for historical reference.

queries.name: All active components by module
queries.description: Lists all registered components grouped by module.
queries.sql:
SELECT module_name, component_name, description
FROM dbo.Component_Registry
WHERE is_active = 1
ORDER BY module_name, component_name;

queries.name: Components with their object counts by category
queries.description: Shows each component with a breakdown of how many objects it contains per category.
queries.sql:
SELECT
    cr.component_name,
    SUM(CASE WHEN oreg.object_category = 'Database' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS db_objects,
    SUM(CASE WHEN oreg.object_category = 'PowerShell' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ps_scripts,
    SUM(CASE WHEN oreg.object_category = 'WebAsset' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS web_assets,
    SUM(CASE WHEN oreg.object_category = 'Documentation' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS doc_files,
    COUNT(oreg.registry_id) AS total
FROM dbo.Component_Registry cr
LEFT JOIN dbo.Object_Registry oreg
    ON oreg.component_name = cr.component_name AND oreg.is_active = 1
WHERE cr.is_active = 1
GROUP BY cr.component_name
ORDER BY cr.component_name;

relationshipNotes.note: Object_Registry has a foreign key to Component_Registry on component_name. Every object in the platform is linked to exactly one component through this relationship.
System_Metadata has a foreign key to Component_Registry on component_name. Version history entries are recorded against the component, not individual objects.

tables.name: Credentials
tables.description: Secure credential storage table containing encrypted configuration values for external service authentication. Used by PowerShell scripts and procedures to retrieve API keys, tokens, and connection strings without hardcoding sensitive data.
tables.dataFlow: Rows are manually inserted with encrypted VARBINARY values using application-layer encryption with a two-tier passphrase model (master passphrase decrypts a service-specific passphrase, which decrypts individual credential values). PowerShell scripts query at runtime to retrieve API tokens, usernames, and passwords for external service integrations (Jira, Teams, SFTP) without hardcoding sensitive data. Decryption occurs in the consuming script, not at the database level.

columns.name: Environment
columns.description: Environment identifier (DEV, TEST, PROD)

columns.name: ServiceName
columns.description: Service this credential belongs to (FK to CredentialServices)

columns.name: ConfigKey
columns.description: Specific credential key (e.g., ApiToken, Username, Password)

columns.name: ConfigValue
columns.description: Encrypted credential value stored as VARBINARY. Decrypted at runtime by consuming PowerShell scripts using the two-tier passphrase model. Contains API keys, tokens, passwords, or connection strings depending on the ConfigKey.

columns.name: CreatedDate
columns.description: When the credential was created

columns.name: ModifiedDate
columns.description: When the credential was last updated

designNotes.note: Credentials use ENCRYPTBYPASSPHRASE with a two-tier key hierarchy: a master passphrase decrypts a service-specific passphrase stored as a ConfigKey = 'Passphrase' row, which in turn decrypts the actual credential values (Username, Password, ApiToken). This provides key rotation at the service level without re-encrypting all credentials.

The three-column composite key (Environment, ServiceName, ConfigKey) allows the same service to have different credentials for DEV, TEST, and PROD environments in a single table. Scripts filter by Environment = 'PROD' at query time.

relationshipNotes.note: Child table. ServiceName references CredentialServices.ServiceName. CredentialServices defines the catalog of valid services; Credentials stores the actual encrypted values per environment.
FileOps.ServerConfig.credential_service_name references the same ServiceName values, linking SFTP server configurations to their authentication credentials.

tables.name: CredentialServices
tables.description: Reference table defining external services that require stored credentials. Provides metadata about each service type and serves as the parent lookup for the Credentials table.
tables.dataFlow: Rows are manually inserted when a new external service integration is established. Serves as the parent lookup table for Credentials, enforcing valid ServiceName values through referential integrity. The ServiceType column categorizes integrations for reporting (API, Webhook, Database, FileShare, Email).

columns.name: ServiceName
columns.description: Unique identifier for the service (e.g., JiraAPI, TeamsWebhook)

columns.name: ServiceType
columns.description: Category of external integration. Classifies services for reporting and grouping: API, Webhook, Database, FileShare, Email.

columns.name: Description
columns.description: Human-readable description of the service purpose

columns.name: Is_Active
columns.description: Whether credentials for this service should be used

columns.name: Created_Date
columns.description: When the service was registered

designNotes.note: This table centralizes the list of all external integrations in one place, making it easy to understand the platform's integration footprint. Deactivating a service here (Is_Active = 0) does not automatically prevent credential retrieval but signals that the integration should not be used.

statusValues.meaning: REST or SOAP API integration (e.g., JiraAPI, ServiceNow).
Outbound webhook notification targets (e.g., TeamsWebhook).
External database connections (e.g., LinkedServer, Oracle).
Network file share or SFTP access (e.g., SFTPServer).
Email service credentials (e.g., SMTPRelay, Office365).

relationshipNotes.note: Parent table. Credentials.ServiceName references CredentialServices.ServiceName. Each service can have multiple credential rows (one per Environment + ConfigKey combination).

tables.name: DatabaseRegistry
tables.description: Registry of databases enrolled in xFACts operations, linking databases to their host servers. This is a shared infrastructure table providing database identification for all modules.
tables.dataFlow: Rows are manually inserted when enrolling a database for xFACts operations. The server_id foreign key links to ServerRegistry. Monitoring scripts query this table joined to ServerRegistry to determine which databases to process. Module-specific configuration tables (e.g., ServerOps.DatabaseConfig) link via database_id for component settings like backup, index maintenance, and statistics preferences.

columns.name: database_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the database enrollment

columns.name: server_id
columns.description: FK to ServerRegistry.server_id

columns.name: database_name
columns.description: Database name as it appears in sys.databases

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Whether this enrollment is active

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When the enrollment was created

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who created the enrollment

columns.name: modified_dttm
columns.description: When the enrollment was last modified

columns.name: modified_by
columns.description: Who last modified the enrollment

designNotes.note: This table contains only identification and server linkage. Component-specific settings live in dedicated configuration tables (ServerOps.DatabaseConfig, etc.), keeping the shared registry focused on identity. This separation was created when the original ServerOps.DatabaseRegistry was split during the dbo schema refactoring.
Databases must be explicitly enrolled rather than auto-discovered. This prevents accidental operations on vendor databases or systems not ready for automated management.

queries.name: All enrolled databases with server info
queries.description: Shows all active database enrollments joined to their host server.
queries.sql:
SELECT d.database_id, d.database_name, s.server_name, s.environment,
       d.is_active, d.notes
FROM dbo.DatabaseRegistry d
JOIN dbo.ServerRegistry s ON s.server_id = d.server_id
WHERE d.is_active = 1
ORDER BY s.server_name, d.database_name;

relationshipNotes.note: Child table. server_id references ServerRegistry.server_id. Each database belongs to exactly one server.
One-to-one extension. ServerOps.DatabaseConfig.database_id references DatabaseRegistry.database_id for component-specific settings (backup, index, statistics configuration).

tables.name: GlobalConfig
tables.description: Consolidated key-value configuration table for all xFACts modules. Stores settings that control component behavior, thresholds, paths, and operational parameters.
tables.dataFlow: Rows are manually inserted or updated when configuring module behavior. Every PowerShell monitoring script and the Control Center read settings at startup or per-cycle using module_name and setting_name lookups. The Control Center GlobalConfig editor page provides a UI for modifying values, with every change logged to dbo.ActionAuditLog as entity_type = 'GlobalConfig'. The is_ui_editable flag controls which settings appear in the editor.

columns.name: config_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the setting

columns.name: module_name
columns.description: Module that owns this setting (ServerOps, dbo, JobFlow, etc.)

columns.name: setting_name
columns.description: Setting identifier (must be unique within module)

columns.name: setting_value
columns.description: The configuration value (stored as string)

columns.name: data_type
columns.description: How to interpret the value: INT, DECIMAL, BIT, VARCHAR

columns.name: category
columns.description: Component within module (Index, Backup, Activity_XE, Activity_DMV, Disk)

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Whether this setting is currently in effect

columns.name: description
columns.description: What this setting controls

columns.name: is_ui_editable
columns.description: Determines whether config is available for editing in Control Center UI

columns.name: notes
columns.description: Additional context or reason for current value

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When the setting was created

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who created the setting

designNotes.note: GlobalConfig replaced four separate configuration tables (ServerOps.Activity_Config, Backup_Config, Disk_Config, Maintenance_Config) during the dbo schema refactoring. The consolidated design provides a single query pattern across all modules while module_name and category maintain logical separation.

All values are stored as VARCHAR in setting_value with a data_type column (INT, DECIMAL, BIT, VARCHAR) indicating how to interpret the value. Consuming code is responsible for casting. This avoids multiple typed columns while preserving type intent for the Control Center editor.

The is_ui_editable flag determines which settings appear in the Control Center GlobalConfig editor. Settings that should only be changed with direct database access (e.g., structural configuration) can be hidden from the UI while remaining queryable by scripts.

queries.name: All settings for a module
queries.description: Shows all active configuration for a specific module with category grouping.
queries.sql:
SELECT setting_name, setting_value, data_type, category, description
FROM dbo.GlobalConfig
WHERE module_name = 'ServerOps'
  AND is_active = 1
ORDER BY category, setting_name;

queries.name: Search for a setting by keyword
queries.description: Finds settings across all modules matching a keyword pattern.
queries.sql:
SELECT module_name, category, setting_name, setting_value, description
FROM dbo.GlobalConfig
WHERE setting_name LIKE '%threshold%'
  AND is_active = 1
ORDER BY module_name, setting_name;

queries.name: UI-editable settings
queries.description: Shows settings available for editing in the Control Center.
queries.sql:
SELECT module_name, category, setting_name, setting_value, data_type, description
FROM dbo.GlobalConfig
WHERE is_ui_editable = 1
  AND is_active = 1
ORDER BY module_name, category, setting_name;

relationshipNotes.note: Every Control Center edit to a GlobalConfig setting generates an ActionAuditLog row with entity_type = 'GlobalConfig' and entity_name = the setting_name, capturing old_value and new_value for audit trail.
The orchestrator engine reads heartbeat_interval_seconds and orchestrator_drain_mode from GlobalConfig on every cycle. Multiple module scripts read their own settings at startup.

tables.name: Holiday
tables.description: Calendar of company holiday dates used by scheduling components across xFACts modules. Contains the list of recognized holidays; actual schedule behaviors are defined in module-specific tables.
tables.dataFlow: Populated via sp_GenerateHolidays for standard annual US holidays (fixed and floating) or sp_AddHoliday for individual company-specific entries. Both procedures apply weekend observation rules (Saturday to Friday, Sunday to Monday). Scheduling-aware modules query this table to determine if the current date is a holiday. Currently consumed by the ServerOps Index component via ServerOps.HolidaySchedule for maintenance window determination.

columns.name: holiday_date
columns.description: Calendar date of the holiday (use observed date for weekend holidays)

columns.name: holiday_name
columns.description: Display name (e.g., "Christmas Day", "Thanksgiving", "Memorial Day")

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Whether this holiday is currently recognized for scheduling purposes

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When this holiday was added

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who added this holiday

designNotes.note: The holiday_date column is the primary key. Each calendar date can appear at most once, preventing duplicate entries. This means the observed date is stored, not the actual holiday date when they differ due to weekend observation.

This table contains only the holiday calendar. How each module behaves on holidays is defined in module-specific tables (e.g., ServerOps.HolidaySchedule). This separation allows different modules to react differently to the same holiday.

queries.name: Upcoming holidays
queries.description: Shows the next 12 months of active holidays.
queries.sql:
SELECT holiday_date, DATENAME(WEEKDAY, holiday_date) AS day_of_week, holiday_name
FROM dbo.Holiday
WHERE holiday_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(YEAR, 1, GETDATE())
  AND is_active = 1
ORDER BY holiday_date;

queries.name: Check if today is a holiday
queries.description: Quick check used by scheduling logic.
queries.sql:
SELECT holiday_name
FROM dbo.Holiday
WHERE holiday_date = CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)
  AND is_active = 1;

relationshipNotes.note: Bulk population procedure that calculates and inserts standard US holidays for a given year, including floating holidays (Memorial Day, Thanksgiving) and weekend observation shifts.
Single-holiday insertion procedure for company-specific holidays or one-off closures not covered by the annual generation.
Defines per-database maintenance window behavior on holidays. References Holiday dates to determine whether index maintenance should run, and with what time window, on each holiday.

tables.name: Module_Registry
tables.description: Top-level module definitions for the xFACts platform. Each module represents a functional domain (ServerOps, JobFlow, Teams, etc.). Completes the three-tier hierarchy: Module_Registry ? Component_Registry ? Object_Registry. The description column holds a brief business-friendly tagline displayed in the Control Center admin panel.
tables.dataFlow: Rows are inserted when new functional modules are established. Referenced by Component_Registry, Object_Registry, and System_Metadata via FK on module_name. The Admin page System Metadata modal reads Module_Registry to display module taglines on the tree header rows.

columns.name: module_id
columns.description: Auto-incrementing primary key.

columns.name: module_name
columns.description: Unique module identifier matching schema names where applicable: dbo, ServerOps, JobFlow, BatchOps, BIDATA, FileOps, Teams, Jira, Orchestrator, ControlCenter, DeptOps.

columns.name: description
columns.description: Business-friendly tagline, 8 words or less. Displayed on the module header row in the System Metadata admin panel.

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Soft delete flag. 1 = active module, 0 = retired.

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When this module was registered. Auto-populated via default.

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who registered this module. Auto-populated via SUSER_SNAME() default.

designNotes.summary: Completes the Module ? Component ? Object model.
designNotes.note: Module_Registry defines the top-level functional domains. Component_Registry groups related objects within a module. Object_Registry catalogs individual assets. System_Metadata tracks version history at the component level. module_name is the natural join key across all four tables.

designNotes.summary: Why the description column is VARCHAR(100).
designNotes.note: The description is displayed inline on the module header row in the admin panel, filling the visual gap between the module name and the component/object counts. Longer descriptions would wrap or truncate. The 8-words-or-less guideline keeps taglines scannable at a glance.

statusValues.meaning: Module is active. Default value on INSERT.
Module has been retired or decommissioned.

queries.name: All active modules
queries.description: Lists all registered modules with their taglines.
queries.sql:
SELECT module_name, description
FROM dbo.Module_Registry
WHERE is_active = 1
ORDER BY module_name;

relationshipNotes.note: Component_Registry has a foreign key to Module_Registry on module_name. Every component must belong to a registered module.
Object_Registry has a foreign key to Module_Registry on module_name. Provides direct module lookup without joining through Component_Registry.
System_Metadata has a foreign key to Module_Registry on module_name. Version entries reference the module for grouping in the admin tree.

tables.name: Object_Metadata
tables.description: Single source of truth for all documentation metadata about database objects across the xFACts platform. Replaces extended properties as the documentation content source. Fed into the DDL JSON export by sp_GenerateDDLReference, rendered automatically on reference and troubleshooting pages.
tables.dataFlow: Populated manually via INSERT/UPDATE during object creation and documentation maintenance. Read by sp_GenerateDDLReference during JSON export to produce schema-level DDL JSON files. Those JSON files are consumed by ddl-loader.js on reference pages and the troubleshooting page in the Control Center documentation site.

columns.name: metadata_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each metadata row

columns.name: schema_name
columns.description: Schema the documented object belongs to: dbo, ServerOps, JobFlow, BatchOps, etc.

columns.name: object_name
columns.description: Name of the documented object: table name, procedure name, script filename, etc.

columns.name: object_type
columns.description: Kind of object: Table, Procedure, Trigger, Function, View, Script

columns.name: column_name
columns.description: NULL for object-level properties. Populated for column-level descriptions and status values scoped to specific columns. For status values applying to multiple columns, use comma-separated column names.

columns.name: property_type
columns.description: What kind of documentation content this row holds. Controls how the export proc and loader handle the row.

columns.name: sort_order
columns.description: Display ordering within a property type for a given object. 0-based. Column descriptions use ordinal position. Design notes, queries, and status values use logical sequence.

columns.name: title
columns.description: Context-dependent label. Query name for queries, topic name for design notes, status value string for status_value rows. NULL for types that do not need a label (description, data_flow, module, category).

columns.name: description
columns.description: Optional short explanation providing context for the content. Used for queries (what the query shows) and design notes (brief summary). NULL when content is self-explanatory.

columns.name: content
columns.description: The actual documentation content. A description paragraph, a full SQL query, a status value meaning, a data flow narrative. Content type is determined by property_type.

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Soft delete flag. Inactive rows are excluded from JSON export. Use this instead of DELETE to preserve audit trail.

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When the row was created

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who created the row (auto-populated from login)

columns.name: modified_dttm
columns.description: When the row was last updated

columns.name: modified_by
columns.description: Who last updated the row (auto-populated from login)

columns.name: column_name_key
columns.description: Persisted computed column: ISNULL(column_name, ''). Provides NULL-safe indexing for the unique filtered index UX_Object_Metadata_NaturalKey.

indexes.name: UX_Object_Metadata_NaturalKey
indexes.keyColumns: schema_name, object_name, object_type, column_name_key, property_type, sort_order

checkConstraints.name: CK_Object_Metadata_ObjectType
checkConstraints.definition: ([object_type]='DDL Trigger' OR [object_type]='XE Session' OR [object_type]='Script' OR [object_type]='View' OR [object_type]='Function' OR [object_type]='Trigger' OR [object_type]='Procedure' OR [object_type]='Table')

checkConstraints.name: CK_Object_Metadata_PropertyType
checkConstraints.definition: ([property_type]='relationship_note' OR [property_type]='status_value' OR [property_type]='query' OR [property_type]='design_note' OR [property_type]='data_flow' OR [property_type]='category' OR [property_type]='module' OR [property_type]='description')

designNotes.topic: Single Source for All Documentation Content
designNotes.note: All documentation metadata lives in this table — object descriptions, column descriptions, design rationale, operational queries, status definitions, and relationship context. Extended properties (MS_Description) are no longer used. One system, one place to look, one way to update.

The DDL export still reads structural metadata (columns, types, constraints, indexes, FKs) from system catalog views. Object_Metadata provides the documentation layer on top. If a column lacks a description row here, it still appears on the reference page with its structural info — just without commentary. Incomplete is better than incorrect.
Rows are deactivated via is_active = 0 rather than deleted. This preserves audit trail and allows reactivation if content is retired prematurely.

A unique filtered index (UX_Object_Metadata_NaturalKey) enforces one active row per natural key combination: schema_name, object_name, object_type, column_name_key, property_type, and sort_order. Scoped to is_active = 1 so deactivated rows do not block new inserts. The column_name_key computed column converts NULL to empty string for clean index behavior since column_name is NULL for object-level rows but populated for column-level rows.

statusValues.meaning: Object or column description. When column_name is NULL, describes the object. When column_name is populated, describes that specific column.
Which module owns this object. Values match schema names: dbo, ServerOps, JobFlow, BatchOps, BIDATA, FileOps, Teams, Jira, Orchestrator, DeptOps.
Functional grouping within a module. Examples: Backup, Index, Activity_XE, Activity_DMV, Disk, Replication for ServerOps. Shared Infrastructure, RBAC for dbo.
Paragraph describing how data enters, moves through, and exits this object. Names the scripts that write to it, the processes that read from it, and what the Control Center displays from it.
Explanation of a non-obvious architectural or design decision. Title holds the topic name. Content holds the rationale.
Common operational query. Title holds the query name. Description holds what the query shows or when to use it. Content holds the full copy-paste-ready SQL.

Definition of a valid status or type value for a check-constrained column. Title holds the value itself. Column_name identifies which column(s) it applies to. Content holds what the value means and when it is set.
Cross-object relationship context that foreign key metadata alone does not convey. Title holds the related object name. Content explains the operational relationship.

queries.name: All metadata for a specific object
queries.description: View everything documented about a single table, procedure, or script.
queries.sql:
SELECT property_type, column_name, sort_order, title, description, content
FROM dbo.Object_Metadata
WHERE schema_name = 'ServerOps'
  AND object_name = 'Backup_FileTracking'
  AND is_active = 1
ORDER BY property_type, sort_order;

queries.name: Objects missing column descriptions
queries.description: Find columns that exist in the database but have no description row in Object_Metadata.
queries.sql:
SELECT s.name AS schema_name, t.name AS table_name, c.name AS column_name
FROM sys.columns c
INNER JOIN sys.tables t ON t.object_id = c.object_id
INNER JOIN sys.schemas s ON s.schema_id = t.schema_id
WHERE s.name IN ('dbo','ServerOps','JobFlow','BatchOps','BIDATA','FileOps','Teams','Jira','Orchestrator','DeptOps')
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM dbo.Object_Metadata om
      WHERE om.schema_name = s.name
        AND om.object_name = t.name
        AND om.column_name = c.name
        AND om.property_type = 'description'
        AND om.is_active = 1
  )
ORDER BY s.name, t.name, c.column_id;

queries.name: Documentation coverage by module
queries.description: Summary of how many objects and columns have descriptions per schema.
queries.sql:
SELECT om.schema_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN om.column_name IS NULL AND om.property_type = 'description' THEN om.object_name END) AS objects_documented,
       COUNT(CASE WHEN om.column_name IS NOT NULL AND om.property_type = 'description' THEN 1 END) AS columns_documented,
       COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN om.property_type = 'query' THEN om.object_name END) AS objects_with_queries,
       COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN om.property_type = 'design_note' THEN om.object_name END) AS objects_with_design_notes
FROM dbo.Object_Metadata om
WHERE om.is_active = 1
GROUP BY om.schema_name
ORDER BY om.schema_name;

tables.name: Object_Registry
tables.description: Complete asset inventory of every object in the xFACts platform. Each row represents an individual database object, PowerShell script, Control Center file, or documentation asset, linked to its parent component via component_name. Serves as the definitive catalog of what exists and where it lives.
tables.dataFlow: Rows are inserted when new objects are created during platform development. Bulk-seeded during the versioning rearchitecture with all existing platform objects. The Admin page System Metadata modal reads Object_Registry to show the object catalog for each component. Future potential: documentation pipeline and file consolidation scripts could use object_path as a source-of-truth for file locations.

columns.name: registry_id
columns.description: Auto-incrementing primary key.

columns.name: module_name
columns.description: Functional module this object belongs to: dbo, ServerOps, JobFlow, BatchOps, BIDATA, FileOps, Teams, Jira, Orchestrator, ControlCenter, DeptOps.

columns.name: component_name
columns.description: Parent component this object belongs to. FK to Component_Registry.component_name.

columns.name: object_name
columns.description: Name of the individual object. Database objects use their SQL name (e.g., GlobalConfig, sp_GenerateDDLReference). Files use their filename with extension (e.g., Collect-DMVMetrics.ps1, admin.css).

columns.name: object_category
columns.description: Broad classification: Database, PowerShell, WebAsset, Documentation.

columns.name: object_type
columns.description: Specific object type within its category: Table, Procedure, Trigger, View, Function, Script, Route, API, Module, JavaScript, CSS, HTML.

columns.name: object_path
columns.description: Where to find this object. Schema name for database objects (e.g., dbo, ServerOps). Full filesystem path for files (e.g., E:\xFACts\scripts\collectors\Collect-DMVMetrics.ps1).

columns.name: description
columns.description: Brief description of what this object does.

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Soft delete flag. 1 = active, 0 = retired/dropped.

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When this object was registered. Auto-populated via default.

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who registered this object. Auto-populated via SUSER_SNAME() default.

checkConstraints.name: CK_Object_Registry_type
checkConstraints.definition: ([object_type]='Config' OR [object_type]='Table' OR [object_type]='Procedure' OR [object_type]='Trigger' OR [object_type]='View' OR [object_type]='Function' OR [object_type]='Script' OR [object_type]='Route' OR [object_type]='API' OR [object_type]='Module' OR [object_type]='JavaScript' OR [object_type]='CSS' OR [object_type]='HTML' OR [object_type]='XE Session' OR [object_type]='DDL Trigger')

designNotes.summary: Two-tier classification system for flexible filtering.
designNotes.note: object_category provides broad grouping (Database, PowerShell, WebAsset, Documentation) for high-level filtering. object_type provides specific classification (Table, Procedure, Script, Route, API, JavaScript, CSS, HTML, etc.) for detailed inventory queries. Both are constrained via CHECK constraints to prevent freeform values.

designNotes.summary: Centralized location tracking for all platform assets.
designNotes.note: Database objects store their schema name in object_path (dbo, ServerOps, etc.). Files store their full filesystem path. This makes Object_Registry the single source of truth for where any object lives, enabling future automation of documentation publishing, file consolidation, and deployment scripts that currently hardcode paths.

designNotes.summary: How objects that serve multiple components are handled.
designNotes.note: Some objects logically participate in multiple components (e.g., Collect-ServerHealth.ps1 serves both ServerOps.ServerHealth and ServerOps.Disk, Send-DiskHealthSummary.ps1 similarly). Each object has one row in Object_Registry linked to its primary component. The description field can note shared usage. This avoids duplication while maintaining a clean one-to-one object-to-component mapping.

statusValues.meaning: SQL Server objects: tables, procedures, triggers, views, functions.
Object is active and in use. Default value on INSERT.
Object has been retired, dropped, or decommissioned. Row preserved for historical reference.
PowerShell scripts (.ps1) and modules (.psm1) in the automation layer.
Control Center files: route pages, API endpoints, JavaScript, CSS.
Documentation site files: HTML pages, doc-specific JS and CSS.

queries.name: All objects for a component
queries.description: Lists every registered object within a specific component.
queries.sql:
SELECT object_name, object_category, object_type, object_path, description
FROM dbo.Object_Registry
WHERE component_name = 'ServerOps.Index'
  AND is_active = 1
ORDER BY object_category, object_type, object_name;

queries.name: Platform inventory by category and type
queries.description: Full breakdown of all active objects across the platform.
queries.sql:
SELECT object_category, object_type, COUNT(*) AS [count]
FROM dbo.Object_Registry
WHERE is_active = 1
GROUP BY object_category, object_type
ORDER BY object_category, object_type;

queries.name: Find an object across the platform
queries.description: Search for an object by partial name match. Useful for finding which component owns a particular script or table.
queries.sql:
SELECT component_name, object_name, object_category, object_type, object_path
FROM dbo.Object_Registry
WHERE object_name LIKE '%BackupStatus%'
  AND is_active = 1;

queries.name: Objects with file paths for a module
queries.description: Lists all file-based objects (scripts, CC files, docs) with their paths for a given module. Useful for deployment and consolidation scripts.
queries.sql:
SELECT component_name, object_name, object_type, object_path
FROM dbo.Object_Registry
WHERE module_name = 'ServerOps'
  AND object_category != 'Database'
  AND is_active = 1
ORDER BY component_name, object_type, object_name;

relationshipNotes.note: Object_Registry has a foreign key to Component_Registry on component_name. Every object must belong to a registered component.

Database objects in Object_Registry should have corresponding Object_Metadata entries for documentation. The object_name and schema (from object_path) map to Object_Metadata.object_name and schema_name respectively.

tables.name: Protection_ViolationLog
tables.description: Audit table capturing all blocked DDL operations on protected xFACts objects. When the protection trigger prevents a DROP or ALTER operation, the attempted action is logged here for security review.
tables.dataFlow: Populated by TR_xFACts_ProtectCriticalObjects via an autonomous transaction through the xFACts_Loopback linked server. When a protected DDL operation is intercepted, the trigger calls sp_LogProtectionViolation through the loopback, which runs in a separate transaction. This ensures the violation is logged even though the trigger then issues a ROLLBACK of the original DDL statement. Rows are append-only.

columns.name: violation_id
columns.description: PK

columns.name: violation_dttm
columns.description: When the blocked DDL operation was attempted. Captured by the protection trigger at the moment of interception.

columns.name: username
columns.description: Windows login of the user who attempted the blocked DDL operation. Captured from the DDL trigger event data.

columns.name: object_name
columns.description: Name of the protected object targeted by the blocked DDL operation. Captured from the DDL trigger event data.

columns.name: event_type
columns.description: Type of DDL operation that was blocked (e.g., DROP_TABLE, ALTER_TABLE, DROP_PROCEDURE). Captured from the DDL trigger event data.

columns.name: sql_text
columns.description: Complete SQL statement that was blocked

designNotes.note: DDL triggers that ROLLBACK face a fundamental problem: any data modifications within the trigger are also rolled back. The loopback linked server pattern (calling sp_LogProtectionViolation via xFACts_Loopback) creates a separate database session. The INSERT commits independently, surviving the trigger's ROLLBACK of the offending DDL.

If the loopback logging fails for any reason, the trigger still blocks the DDL operation. Protection is never compromised by logging failures. The logging is best-effort but the protection is absolute.

queries.name: Recent violations
queries.description: Shows blocked DDL attempts within the last 7 days.
queries.sql:
SELECT violation_id, violation_dttm, username, object_name,
       event_type, LEFT(sql_text, 200) AS sql_preview
FROM dbo.Protection_ViolationLog
WHERE violation_dttm >= DATEADD(DAY, -7, GETDATE())
ORDER BY violation_dttm DESC;

queries.name: Violations by user
queries.description: Summary of blocked operations per user for security review.
queries.sql:
SELECT username, COUNT(*) AS violation_count,
       MIN(violation_dttm) AS first_violation,
       MAX(violation_dttm) AS last_violation
FROM dbo.Protection_ViolationLog
GROUP BY username
ORDER BY violation_count DESC;

relationshipNotes.note: Source. The DDL trigger intercepts protected operations and logs the attempt to this table before issuing ROLLBACK.
The INSERT is performed by this procedure, called through the xFACts_Loopback linked server to ensure the log entry commits in a separate transaction from the rolled-back DDL.

tables.name: RBAC_ActionGrant
tables.description: Action-level permission overrides for the Control Center RBAC framework. Provides fine-grained ALLOW and DENY grants at the role or individual user level, supplementing the tier-based permissions defined in RBAC_ActionRegistry.
tables.dataFlow: Rows are manually inserted when exceptions to the standard tier-based permissions are needed. The RBAC cache loads all active grants at startup. During action permission evaluation, the middleware first checks grant overrides before falling back to tier-based defaults. DENY grants are evaluated before ALLOW grants, and DENY always wins when both exist for the same user/action.

columns.name: grant_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the grant

columns.name: grant_type
columns.description: ALLOW or DENY. DENY always takes precedence over ALLOW

columns.name: grant_scope
columns.description: ROLE (applies to a role) or USER (applies to a specific user)

columns.name: action_id
columns.description: Foreign key to RBAC_ActionRegistry. Identifies the action being granted or denied

columns.name: role_id
columns.description: Foreign key to RBAC_Role. Populated when grant_scope = 'ROLE'

columns.name: username
columns.description: AD username without domain prefix. Populated when grant_scope = 'USER'

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Whether this grant is currently in effect

columns.name: description
columns.description: Explanation of why this grant exists

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When the grant was created

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who created the grant

columns.name: modified_dttm
columns.description: When the grant was last modified

columns.name: modified_by
columns.description: Who last modified the grant

checkConstraints.name: CK_RBAC_ActionGrant_scope_fields
checkConstraints.definition: ([grant_scope]='ROLE' AND [role_id] IS NOT NULL AND [username] IS NULL OR [grant_scope]='USER' AND [username] IS NOT NULL AND [role_id] IS NULL)

designNotes.note: When both ALLOW and DENY exist for the same user/action combination, DENY wins. This implements the principle of least privilege — it is safer to accidentally over-restrict than to accidentally over-permit.

Most grants should be at the ROLE level. User-level grants (grant_scope = 'USER') are the escape hatch for situations where creating a new AD group and role mapping would be overkill. The check constraint enforces that exactly one of role_id or username is populated.

The middleware evaluates: (1) user-level DENY, (2) role-level DENY, (3) user-level ALLOW, (4) role-level ALLOW, (5) tier-based default from RBAC_PermissionMapping + RBAC_ActionRegistry. The first match wins.

statusValues.meaning: Grants access to an action the user would not normally have based on their tier. Example: ReadOnly users get ALLOW for kill-zombie because everyone loves killing zombie connections.
Grant applies to all users who hold the specified role. The standard approach for most overrides.
Grant applies to a specific AD username. The exception path for one-off permissions that do not justify a new AD group.

Revokes access to an action the user would normally have. Always takes precedence over ALLOW. Example: PowerUser gets DENY for bulk-toggle-tasks because bulk operations on production flows are admin-only.

queries.name: All active grants with action details
queries.description: Shows every override with the action it applies to.
queries.sql:
SELECT ag.grant_type, ag.grant_scope, 
       COALESCE(r.role_name, ag.username) AS grantee,
       ar.action_name, ar.page_route, ag.description
FROM dbo.RBAC_ActionGrant ag
JOIN dbo.RBAC_ActionRegistry ar ON ar.action_id = ag.action_id
LEFT JOIN dbo.RBAC_Role r ON r.role_id = ag.role_id
WHERE ag.is_active = 1
ORDER BY ag.grant_type DESC, ar.page_route, ar.action_name;

relationshipNotes.note: Parent table. action_id references RBAC_ActionRegistry.action_id. Identifies which action the grant applies to.
Optional parent. role_id references RBAC_Role.role_id when grant_scope = 'ROLE'. NULL when grant_scope = 'USER'.

tables.name: RBAC_ActionRegistry
tables.description: Registry of protectable actions in the Control Center. Defines which API endpoints require permission checks, what page they belong to, and what tier is required to execute them.
tables.dataFlow: Rows are manually inserted when new protected API endpoints are created in the Control Center. The RBAC cache loads all active actions at startup. When an API route handler executes, it looks up the endpoint path and HTTP method in the cache to find the action's required_tier and page_route. The user's tier for that page (from RBAC_PermissionMapping) determines default access, with RBAC_ActionGrant providing overrides.

columns.name: action_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the action

columns.name: action_name
columns.description: Business key for the action (e.g., 'kill-zombie', 'toggle-task'). Used in audit logs and ActionGrant references

columns.name: api_endpoint
columns.description: Full API path (e.g., '/api/server-health/kill-zombies')

columns.name: http_method
columns.description: HTTP method: POST, PUT, or DELETE

columns.name: page_route
columns.description: Parent page route for tier resolution (e.g., '/server-health')

columns.name: required_tier
columns.description: Minimum tier required: admin, operate, or view

columns.name: description
columns.description: What this action does

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Whether this action is currently enforced

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When the action was registered

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who registered the action

columns.name: modified_dttm
columns.description: When the action was last modified

columns.name: modified_by
columns.description: Who last modified the action

designNotes.note: Before this table existed, action permission checks required hardcoded parameters at every API endpoint. Adding a new protected action meant code changes. With the registry, adding a protected endpoint is a single INSERT and the route handler only needs one line of code that reads everything else from the cached registry.

The combination of api_endpoint and http_method uniquely identifies an action. The route handler looks up permission requirements from $WebEvent.Path and $WebEvent.Method without knowing anything about the action's name or tier. action_name serves as the human-readable business key used in audit logs and ActionGrant overrides.

statusValues.meaning: Only users with admin tier on the parent page can execute. Used for dangerous or configuration-level actions.
Users with operate or admin tier can execute. The default for most standard workflow actions.
Any user with access to the page can execute. Used for actions that are safe for everyone, typically paired with ALLOW grants for broader access.

queries.name: All registered actions by page
queries.description: Shows every protected API endpoint grouped by parent page.
queries.sql:
SELECT page_route, action_name, api_endpoint, http_method, required_tier, description
FROM dbo.RBAC_ActionRegistry
WHERE is_active = 1
ORDER BY page_route, action_name;

relationshipNotes.note: Child table. RBAC_ActionGrant.action_id references RBAC_ActionRegistry.action_id for ALLOW/DENY overrides on specific actions.
Logical relationship. page_route values match entries in RBAC_PermissionMapping. The user's tier on the parent page determines their default access to the action.
action_name from this table appears in RBAC_AuditLog entries when action-level permission checks are logged.

tables.name: RBAC_AuditLog
tables.description: Logs permission evaluation events from the Control Center RBAC framework. Captures access denials, authentication events, and optionally all permission checks for compliance and troubleshooting.
tables.dataFlow: Populated by the Control Center RBAC middleware during permission evaluation. Verbosity is controlled by the GlobalConfig setting ControlCenter.rbac_audit_verbosity: 'denials_only' logs only DENIED and WOULD_DENY events, 'all' logs every permission check. Rows are append-only. The ad_groups and resolved_roles columns capture the user's state at event time as denormalized comma-separated strings.

columns.name: audit_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the audit event

columns.name: event_type
columns.description: Type of event (see Event Types below)

columns.name: username
columns.description: AD username of the user. NULL for unauthenticated events

columns.name: ad_groups
columns.description: Comma-separated list of the user's AD groups at time of event

columns.name: resolved_roles
columns.description: Comma-separated list of roles resolved from AD groups

columns.name: page_route
columns.description: Page route being accessed. NULL for login events

columns.name: action_name
columns.description: Action being attempted. NULL for page-level access checks

columns.name: required_tier
columns.description: Minimum tier required for the page/action

columns.name: user_tier
columns.description: User's resolved tier for the page

columns.name: result
columns.description: Outcome: ALLOWED, DENIED, or WOULD_DENY (audit mode)

columns.name: detail
columns.description: Additional context about the decision

columns.name: client_ip
columns.description: Client IP address

columns.name: event_dttm
columns.description: When the event occurred

designNotes.note: The user's AD groups and resolved roles are stored as comma-separated strings rather than normalized references. This captures the exact permission state at the time of the event, preserving accuracy even if roles, group memberships, or mappings change later.

The RBAC framework supports disabled (no checks), audit (checks run but only log, never block), and enforce (checks run and block unauthorized access). During the audit-to-enforce transition, WOULD_DENY events provide visibility into what would be blocked before anyone gets locked out.

API_RequestLog captures all HTTP requests for traffic and performance analysis. RBAC_AuditLog focuses specifically on authorization decisions — who was blocked, why, and what permissions they had. Together they provide complete request + authorization visibility.

statusValues.meaning: User successfully authenticated via Active Directory.
Permission check passed. Action or page access was granted.
Permission check failed in enforce mode. Access was blocked.
Active Directory authentication failed.
User lacks page-level permission. Logged in enforce mode.
Permission check failed in audit mode. Access was allowed but the event was logged for impact assessment.
User would lack page-level permission. Logged in audit mode without blocking.
User lacks action-level permission. Logged in enforce mode.
User would lack action-level permission. Logged in audit mode without blocking.
RBAC table configuration was modified through the admin interface.

queries.name: Recent denials
queries.description: Shows blocked or would-be-blocked events in the last 7 days.
queries.sql:
SELECT event_type, username, page_route, action_name,
       required_tier, user_tier, result, detail, event_dttm
FROM dbo.RBAC_AuditLog
WHERE result IN ('DENIED', 'WOULD_DENY')
  AND event_dttm >= DATEADD(DAY, -7, GETDATE())
ORDER BY event_dttm DESC;

queries.name: Audit mode impact assessment
queries.description: Shows how many users and pages would be affected by enabling enforcement.
queries.sql:
SELECT username, page_route, action_name,
       COUNT(*) AS occurrence_count
FROM dbo.RBAC_AuditLog
WHERE result = 'WOULD_DENY'
  AND event_dttm >= DATEADD(DAY, -7, GETDATE())
GROUP BY username, page_route, action_name
ORDER BY occurrence_count DESC;

relationshipNotes.note: Logical relationship. action_name values in audit entries correspond to RBAC_ActionRegistry.action_name. No physical foreign key since the audit log must persist even if actions are removed.
Complementary table. API_RequestLog captures HTTP request metrics; RBAC_AuditLog captures authorization decisions. Together they provide complete request-level visibility.
The ControlCenter.rbac_audit_verbosity setting controls logging volume. 'denials_only' keeps the table lean during normal operation; 'all' provides complete audit coverage when needed.

tables.name: RBAC_DepartmentRegistry
tables.description: Registry of departmental pages in the Control Center. Maps department identifiers to their page routes and display names.
